purpose: Positive writing moves for Lumi AI Daily. These are examples of editorial transformations, not templates to copy.

style_lock.all_text_surfaces: Title, cover, WeChat, video screen copy, narration, and Bilibili should share the same daily thesis while adapting to their platform.
style_lock.core_chain: concrete daily change -> plain-language problem -> technical mechanism -> product, cost, workflow, control, or reliability consequence
style_lock.term_rule: A hard term should arrive after the reader has seen the practical problem it names.

editorial_moves.name: scene_to_signal
editorial_moves.when_to_use: A product or model update changes how users work.
editorial_moves.input_shape: Company X released feature Y; source says it improves Z.
editorial_moves.output_move: Begin with the work scene that changes, then name the product and explain why the change matters.
editorial_moves.sample: 以前这一步像在等模型给一张答案纸，现在更像看一个工具在你面前把活拆开做。这个差别，才是这次产品更新真正有价值的地方。

editorial_moves.name: mechanism_before_judgment
editorial_moves.when_to_use: A research item could otherwise become abstract.
editorial_moves.input_shape: Paper proposes method A to reduce cost B.
editorial_moves.output_move: Explain the bottleneck first, then explain the method's idea, then state the implication.
editorial_moves.sample: 长上下文贵，不只是因为窗口变大，而是模型推理时要一路带着越来越多的记忆。这个工作动的不是外围调度，而是记忆本身的表示方式。

editorial_moves.name: term_bridge_before_jargon
editorial_moves.when_to_use: A section contains a hard term, acronym, architecture name, benchmark, chip, model card detail, or paper method.
editorial_moves.input_shape: The fact draft contains terms such as KV cache, dense model, VRAM, agentic, tool calling, Ascend, or context window.
editorial_moves.output_move: Write the plain problem first, then introduce the term as the name of that problem, then explain what changes for a concrete reader.
editorial_moves.sample: 模型读很长的材料时，不是看完就忘，它要把前文留下的线索一路带到后面。工程里这部分记忆通常会变成 KV cache；它越大，推理越贵，所以长上下文真正难的不是窗口写多大，而是这份记忆能不能被便宜地带着走。

editorial_moves.name: ordinary_reader_ladder
editorial_moves.when_to_use: The story has real technical depth but the first paragraph may lose non-specialist readers.
editorial_moves.input_shape: A model, paper, chip, or infrastructure update needs explanation before judgment.
editorial_moves.output_move: Use three steps: everyday work scene, system bottleneck, technical mechanism. End with one consequence in product or business language.
editorial_moves.sample: 如果一个 coding agent 只是改一行代码，它像聊天；如果它要读完整仓库、跑测试、反复修 bug，它就开始像一个小型工程流程。这里的瓶颈不只是模型会不会写代码，而是上下文能不能装得下、工具调用能不能稳、失败后能不能继续推进。

editorial_moves.name: cost_or_control_transfer
editorial_moves.when_to_use: Infrastructure, open-source, chip, cloud, or platform news.
editorial_moves.input_shape: A model supports hardware, deployment path, or platform integration.
editorial_moves.output_move: Name which cost or control point is moving, and who gains room to choose.
editorial_moves.sample: 它真正松动的不是一项参数，而是采购和部署时的默认路线。对企业来说，多一条能跑通的算力路径，就多一点议价和架构主动权。

editorial_moves.name: reader_role_anchor
editorial_moves.when_to_use: A story risks sounding like general industry commentary.
editorial_moves.input_shape: The news may affect developers, researchers, founders, product teams, or operators.
editorial_moves.output_move: Pick the first affected reader role and write the consequence from their desk.
editorial_moves.sample: 最先感到变化的不是普通聊天用户，而是每天要把模型接进代码库、工单和内部工具的团队。对他们来说，稳定比惊艳更值钱。

editorial_moves.name: whole_day_compression
editorial_moves.input_shape: Six stories point to a common direction.
editorial_moves.output_move: Return to a concrete detail from the opening, then let that detail reveal the single frame behind the day.
editorial_moves.sample: 开头那句“先给用户一个可见更新”，最后看起来不像提示词小技巧，而像 agent 产品成熟的起点：可靠的系统会让人知道它在做什么，也会让团队看清它能被放进哪一段工作。

editorial_moves.name: platform_same_thesis_not_same_sentence
editorial_moves.when_to_use: Writing Bilibili, video screen copy, or narration after the WeChat article direction is known.
editorial_moves.input_shape: A polished daily thesis and six story roles exist.
editorial_moves.output_move: Keep the same core judgment but rewrite for the surface: title uses conflict, cover uses visual compression, screen cards use factual bite, narration uses spoken transitions, platform copy uses click reason.
editorial_moves.sample: 同一条主线，在公众号里可以展开成系统能力；到封面上只剩“GPT重写协作”；到口播里则变成一句能听懂的话：模型开始不只是回答，而是要让人看见它怎么把活做完。

editorial_moves.name: cold_open_without_meta_news
editorial_moves.when_to_use: Opening narration or article lead feels like a daily report template.
editorial_moves.input_shape: The draft begins with today, this group of news, what I remembered first, or a not-A-but-B contrast.
editorial_moves.output_move: Remove the meta frame and start with the concrete product/user/engineering friction, then reveal why this detail matters.
editorial_moves.sample: 一个 agent 沉默几十秒，比答错更容易让人不安：你不知道它是在拆任务、调工具，还是已经跑偏。GPT-5.5 这次真正有意思的地方，就藏在这个等待过程里。

editorial_moves.name: subject_first_screen_title
editorial_moves.when_to_use: A video page title is abstract or missing the actor.
editorial_moves.input_shape: Title or top progress label says 'long context pressure returns to attention structure' or 'workflow changes'.
editorial_moves.output_move: Put the company/model/paper/project/platform first, then describe the change; for tight progress labels, use a recognizable subject abbreviation plus the hook.
editorial_moves.sample: Tensor Product Attention 把长上下文压力带回注意力结构。

editorial_moves.name: lead_card_plus_support_cards
editorial_moves.when_to_use: Video information cards look equal, flat, or low-density.
editorial_moves.input_shape: Three cards have similar length, same weight, and all summarize the story.
editorial_moves.output_move: Make card one the core change, card two the mechanism, and card three the consequence; let card one carry more weight and the others read faster.
editorial_moves.sample: 主卡写“Feldera反拟人”，辅卡写“接口先行”和“边界可观察”，观众先知道核心冲突，再知道机制和影响。

rhythm_guidance: Avoid making every section start with the same contrast pattern.
Use concrete nouns before abstract nouns.
Let one paragraph do one job: scene, mechanism, consequence, or judgment.
Hard terms should arrive after a plain-language problem, not as the first explanation.
When a section uses an acronym or architecture term, include a reader checkpoint that translates it into cost, workflow, control, reliability, or adoption.
Use bold only for key entities or the one concept that helps scanning.
If two adjacent sections use the same opening rhythm, rewrite the later one from another angle.
Openings should not announce the existence of a news list; they should enter a scene.
Video page titles and top progress labels should start with the visible actor, not an abstract theme.
Screen cards need visual and semantic hierarchy: lead, mechanism, consequence.
